audited_agent_message: CLEARSTEM: I'm sorry about that. Let me resend it. My message goes like this: "Thanks for your patience! I just heard from the team. Thanks for reaching out to us! Communication is everything! It sounds like there's a missing piece in the puzzle, and we are here to help! Our products have been tested rigorously, but everyone's skin is unique, and we want you to understand yours better. Here are the big questions to ask yourself that contribute to acne that very often get overlooked Please read and digest them thoroughly and take each one seriously, or you may never get to a place of peace with your skin. It should take you about 20 minutes to really analyze these factors and double-check all your skincare, makeup, and vitamins. 1. Have you completely removed all pore-clogging ingredients from your makeup, lotions, other skincare products, and hair products? This is step 1 and it is absolutely crucial or you will just be re-clogging your pores. Here is our Pore Clogger Checker https://clearstem.attn.tv/aW2QMXzbqx4F! 2. Are you using any retinols or tretinoin/ Retin-A? We generally advise against using these (or just take a long break) because they are notorious for causing long-lasting purging and high irritation. The white cream Retin-A actually has isopropyl myristate which is a major pore-clogger. 3. Are you consistently using VITAMINSCUB and CLEARITY and allowing CLEARITY to fully penetrate for at least 10-15 minutes? If we do not let CLEARITY sit by itself for 10-15 minutes it will not have enough time to unclog the pore. 4. Do any of your vitamins have excessive B12, excess Vitamin D, excess Zinc, or high levels of Biotin? We recommend staying below 125% RDV for these nutrients in supplement form to eliminate variables (unless expressly directed by your physician) 5. Are you supplementing with a vitamin product that has a lot of Ashwaganda or Rhodiola in it? (Think of green juices or "adaptogen" items). These can spike testosterone and cause hormonal acne in some people. 6. How often are you consuming eggs, soy, whey protein, gluten, or dairy? These will all contribute to acne"
